ncert_truth_statements: In a parallel combination, the potential difference across each resistor is the same and equal to the applied voltage
The reciprocal of the equivalent resistance equals the sum of reciprocals of individual resistances: 1/R = 1/R1 + 1/R2
The total current drawn from the source equals the sum of the currents through each parallel branch
In a parallel combination, the equivalent resistance is always less than the resistance of the smallest individual resistor
Current through each branch is inversely proportional to its resistance when the same voltage is applied across all branches
